revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Ikagashikoo]][[ja:伊香色雄]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|spouse={{ill|Q106111981|qid=Q106111981|1=Maki-hime-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q106111988|qid=Q106111988|1=Arabi no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q106112002|qid=Q106112002|1=Tamatehime no Mikoto}}|children={{ill|Q106107729|qid=Q106107729|1=Ominakuchi-hime}}; {{ill|Q106534482|qid=Q106534482|1=Oamefu no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q108044745|qid=Q108044745|1=Niikawa-hime no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q108933353|qid=Q108933353|1=Oaragawa no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q11436785|qid=Q11436785|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune}}; {{ill|Q24885356|qid=Q24885356|1=Mononobe no Tokone}}|father={{ill|Ōhesoki|qid=Q106106718|1=Ōhesoki}}}}
{{infobox royalty| | father = {{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜麻杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=}}<ref name="nakatomi" /> | 1father = '''{{ill|Mimo no sukune|ja|御物宿祢|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimo no sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' | 1issue = '''{{ill|Iso no Sukune|ja|五十野宿祢|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iso no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491578|1=Iso no Sukune}}''', {{ill|Yashiro no Sukune|ja|矢代宿祢|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Shinto | image = File:Isonokami.jpg | 1caption = He was the first priest of [[Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]] |house={{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}|p2redecessor={{ill|Nunaki-iri-hime|ja|渟名城入姫命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|qq=}}|su2ccessor='''{{ill|Iso no Sukune|ja|五十野宿祢|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iso no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491578|1=Iso no Sukune}}'''|issue={{ill|Mononobe no Toochine|ja|物部十千根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Toochine|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24885356}}|caption=His son would be given {{ill|Isonokami Jingu|lt=Isonokami Jingu|lt=Isonokami Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Shrine|qq=}} which became the Mononobe clan shrine dedicated to {{ill|Futsu-no-mitama|ja|布都御魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsu-no-mitama|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|Nigihayahi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}|successor={{ill|Mononobe no Toochine|ja|物部十千根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Toochine|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24885356}}}}
{{Nihongo|{{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikoo|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o}} was a Japanese mythological figure.<ref name="Chamberlain-Basil-1882a" /> His sister was {{ill|Ikagashikome|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikome|qq=}}.
== Mythical narrative == During Emperor Sujin's reign, a major epidemic and political crisis<ref name="aston150–164">{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ|title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 2|author=Aston, William George.|date=1896|publisher=The Japan Society London|isbn=9780524053478|pages=150–164|author-link=William George Aston|qq=draft}}</ref> This led tim ho make major religious reforms to resolve it.<ref name="aston150–164" /> <ref name="aston150–164" /><ref name="Ellwood-1990a">{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood|first=Robert S.|date=1990|title=The Sujin Religious Revolution|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/30234018|journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies|volume=17|issue=2/3|pages=199–217|doi=10.18874/jjrs.17.2-3.1990.199-217|issn=0304-1042|jstor=30234018}}</ref> .<ref name="aston150–164" />
Out of an abundance of caution, the Emperor also appointed {{Nihongo|{{ill|Ikagashikoo|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikoo|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o}} as {{Nihongo|''kami-no-mono-akatsu-hito''|神班物者}}, or one who sorts the offerings to the gods.<ref name="Chamberlain-Basil-1882a">{{cite book |author=Chamberlain, Basil |url=http://www.sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj072.htm |title=''[SECT. LXV.—EMPEROR SŪ-JIN (PART III: STORY OF OHO-TATA-NE-KO'S BIRTH)]'' (''The Kojiki'') |publisher=Read before the Asiatic Society of Japan on April 12, May 10, and June 21, 1882, reprinted in 1919 |page=219 |quote=His Augustness Oho-tata-ne-ko ... was the ancestor of the Dukes of Miwa and of the Dukes of Kamo. |author-link=Basil Chamberlain}}</ref> {{ill|Ikagashikoo|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikoo|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}} became the ancestor of the now extinct {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}.<ref name="Chamberlain-Basil-1882a" />
He first enshrined {{ill|Futsu-no-mitama|fr|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Futsu-no-Mitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsu-no-mitama|qq=}} at Isonokami Shrine.<ref> https://nippon-kichi.jp/article_list.do?ml_lang=en&p=1390</ref>
Afterwards, the Emperor ordered Ikashikio to make the many hiraka pots, like those of the Mononobe clan, into offerings to the deities (kamimatsurimono, or offerings at divine festivals), and to make Otataneko the chief priest of Omononushi-no-Okami and Ichiisonagaoichi the chief priest of Yamato-Okunitama-no-Kami. As a result, the epidemic subsided, the country became peaceful, the grains grew, and the peasants prospered. <ref>『日本書紀』崇神天皇7th year pp月13日条</ref>
This is the only scene in which Ikashikio appears, but from it we can see that Emperor Sujin implemented a policy of unity of religion and politics by worshiping the god of {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|qid=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa|1=Mount Miwa}} {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=in Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, thereby solidifying the foundation of the Yamato government and beginning to unify the surrounding countries. <ref>中央公論社『日本の歴史1』p281 - 282</ref>
==Overview==
{{Infobox noble | name = Ikagashiko no Mikoto (伊香色雄命) | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ikagashiko no Mikoto, Ikaga Shiko, Ikagashiko no Omikoto, and others | title = | divine_title = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = | Rank = [[Ōomi|Minister]], Kami Mono Akatsuhito (神班物者) | lord = [[Emperor Kaika]], [[Emperor Sujin]] | clan = [[Mononobe clan]] | parents = Father: [[Ōsonomakine no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson">"Tenson Honki," in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}.</ref>, Mother: Takaya Awara-hime<ref name="tenson"/> | siblings = [[Ikagashiko no Mikoto|Ikagashiko]] | spouse = Maki-hime<ref name="tenson"/>, Ara-hime<ref name="tenson"/>, Tamate-hime<ref name="tenson"/>, Matori-hime<ref name="tenson"/> | children = Tatekoshin Ōne no Mikoto<ref name="tenson"/>, Tabe Sukune no Mikoto<ref name="tenson"/>, Amaketa Tami no Mikoto<ref name="tenson"/>, [[Ōarakawa no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/>, [[Mononobe no Totsukane|Totsukane no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/>, Takashinagawa no Mikoto<ref name="tenson"/>, [[Ōume no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/>, Ketsuwake no Mikoto? }}
'''Ikagashiko no Mikoto''' (伊香色雄命) was a [[Kofun period]] nobleman and the progenitor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe lineage]] according to records like the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}. He was appointed "Kami Mono Akatsuhito" (神班物者) to officiate over the worship of {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} in the 7th year of Emperor Sujin's reign. He is described as a sixth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}.<ref name="tenson">{{Sfn|Izumi City History Editorial Committee|1965|p=84}}</ref>
== Records == According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, during Emperor Sujin's reign, a plague caused widespread mortality. In a divine revelation, the deity Ōmononushi appeared in the Emperor's dream, instructing him to appoint Ōtataneko as his priest, promising peace in the land. The Emperor followed this divine command: {{quotation|Ōtataneko was thus appointed as the head priest, worshipping Ōmononushi at Mount Miwa. Additionally, Ikagashiko no Mikoto was commanded to prepare offerings for the gods, thus restoring peace and ending the plague.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=Kojiki: Middle Volume|publisher=小学館|date=1983}}</ref>}}
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} presents a similar account, adding that Emperor Sujin's daughters were instructed to serve {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|Yamato Okunitama|qid=Q11387080|lt=Yamato no Ōkunitama|1=Yamato Okunitama}}.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=Nihon Shoki, Volume V|publisher=岩波文庫|date=1994}}</ref> Ikagashiko no Mikoto was subsequently entrusted with overseeing rituals related to these deities, solidifying his role as a pivotal figure in the early Yamato polity.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=宇治谷孟|title=全現代語訳 日本書紀 上|publisher=講談社学術文庫|date=1988}}</ref>
== Stone Chambers and the Sacred Sword == Ikagashiko is credited with initiating the worship of the sacred sword {{ill|Futsu no Mitama|ja|韴霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsu no Mitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 布都御魂|1=Futsu-no-mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11059754}} at {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|qid=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}, following Emperor Sujin’s directive.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.isonokami.jp/about/c2.html |title=Official Site of Isonokami Shrine |access-date=2024-04-29 }}</ref>
== Genealogy == The father of this lineage is identified as {{ill|Ōsomahiki-no-Mikoto|ja|大綜麻杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsomahiki-no-Mikoto|1=Ōhesoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106106718}}, and the mother is Takaya-Awarahime (高屋阿波良姫). He is said to have had four wives: Maki-hime (真木姫), Ara-hime (荒姫), and Tamate-hime (玉手姫), daughters of the ancestor of the {{ill|Yamashiro Agatanushi|ja|山代県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Agatanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山背国造|1=Yamashiro no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11470858}}, Nagamizo; and Matori-hime (真鳥姫), daughter of {{ill|Yamato-shiki no Hikome|ja|倭志紀彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shiki no Hikome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Through these unions, he fathered numerous progeny, who became ancestors of various clans and lineages.
* '''With Maki-hime (真木姫):''' ** '''Takenokokoro Ōnemi no Mikoto (建胆心大禰命):''' Ancestor of the {{ill|Mononobe no Muraji|ja|物部連公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. ** '''Tabeno Sukune no Mikoto (多辨宿禰命):''' Ancestor of {{ill|Ujibe no Muraji|ja|宇治部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibe no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hata no Imiki|ja|秦忌寸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata no Imiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katsuno|ja|葛野野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuno|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kurisu no Muraji|ja|栗栖連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurisu no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ujiyamamoribe no Muraji|ja|宇治山守連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamamoribe no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Haseyama no Ason|ja|長谷山直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haseyama no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
* '''With Ara-hime (荒姫):''' ** '''Amake Takemi no Mikoto (安毛建美命):''' Ancestor of the Mononobe no Muraji, {{ill|Rokujinabe no Muraji|ja|六人部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujinabe no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minatori no Miyatsuko|ja|水取造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatori no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minatori no Muraji|ja|水取連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatori no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Tsukikome no Sukune|ja|舂米宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukikome no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** '''Ōnikawa no Mikoto (大新河命):''' Ancestor of {{ill|Haseokihajime no Muraji|ja|長谷置始連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haseokihajime no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takahashi no Muraji|ja|高橋連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatabe no Miyatsuko|ja|矢田部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatabe no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yatsudomi no Muraji|ja|矢集連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsudomi no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayabe no Ason|ja|佐夜部直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayabe no Ason|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayabe no Obito|ja|佐夜部首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayabe no Obito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kunu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久努国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85875681}}, {{ill|Takebe|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takebe|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55530004}}, {{ill|Koichi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=ochinokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21652684}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Ochi no Obito|ja|越智氏|Ochi clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi no Obito|1=Ochi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11636555}}), {{ill|Kazehaya no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|風速国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazehaya no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=kazahayanokuninomiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21652739}} (ancestor of the Kazehaya no Obito), and {{ill|Karube no Miyatsuko|ja|軽部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karube no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
* '''With Tamate-hime (玉手姫):''' ** '''Tochinone no Mikoto (十市根命):''' Ancestor of {{ill|Mononobe no Tōjitsu|ja|物部十千根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tōjitsu|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24885356}}. ** '''Takenokawa no Mikoto (建新川命):''' Ancestor of the {{ill|Yamato-shiki Agatanushi|ja|倭志紀県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-shiki Agatanushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōyake no Obito|ja|大宅首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Obito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kawakami no Miyatsuko|ja|川上造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Harumichi no Sukune|ja|春道宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harumichi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
* '''With Matori-hime (真鳥姫):''' ** '''Ōburafu no Mikoto (大売布命):''' Ancestor of the Yamato-shiki Agatanushi (志紀連), {{ill|Tōichibe no Obito|ja|十市部首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōichibe no Obito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久自国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuji Kokuzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369664}}, {{ill|Ōbe no Miyatsuko|ja|大部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbe no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Magakibe no Miyatsuko|ja|真髪部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magakibe no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakayusumaro no Muraji|ja|若湯坐連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayusumaro no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Arimichi no Sukune|ja|有道宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arimichi no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Imaki no Muraji|ja|今木連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Izumi Shiki Agatanushi|ja|和泉志紀県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Shiki Agatanushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, genealogical traditions from sources such as the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} include {{ill|Makandakone no Muraji|ja|真神田曽禰連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makandakone no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Makanda no Obito|ja|真神田首|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makanda no Obito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sone no Muraji|ja|曽根連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, tracing their lineage to a son named {{ill|Kizubetsu no Mikoto|ja|気津別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizubetsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Worship == Ikagashiko is enshrined at {{ill|Ikagashi Shrine|ja|伊加加志神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashi Shrine|1=Ikagashi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378911}}, an ancient shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. It is located in {{ill|Yoshinogawa, Tokushima|en|Yoshinogawa, Tokushima|qid=Q592500|lt=Yoshinogawa|1=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima}}, Tokushima Prefecture.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Mononobe no Totsukane]] * [[List of people before the Asuka period]]
== External Links == * {{Kotobank|2=デジタル版 日本人名大辞典+Plus}}
==Genealogy==
{{Chart/start|align=center|summary=An example family tree<ref name="nakatomi" />}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| }} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|Nigihayahi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}<ref name="nakatomi" />}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| }} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji|lt=Umashimazu-no-Mikoto|en:draft|Umashimaji|lt=Umashimaji|lt=Umashimazu-no-Mikoto|ja|宇摩志麻遅命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimazu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウマシマジ}}<ref name="nakatomi" />}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| }} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Hikoyuki no Mikoto|ja|彦湯支命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoyuki no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref name="nakatomi" />}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| }} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Omine no Mikoto|ja|大禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omine no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="nakatomi" />}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| }} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Izushi Kokoro no Oomi no Mikoto|ja|出石心大臣命|1=Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Oomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izushi Kokoro no Ōomi no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref name="nakatomi" />}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| }} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Ōhesoki|ja|大綜麻杵命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhesoki|qq=}}<ref name="nakatomi" />}} {{Chart| | ||| | | |!| }} {{Chart| | |||| |ILL| | | | |ILL={{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikagashikoo|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}<ref name="nakatomi">{{ill|Kujiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujiki|qq=}}</ref>}} {{Chart/end}}
== In popular culture == In Touhou Lost world he is referenced in a spell card of the character Mononobe no Futo.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Wind Sign: Miwa Plate Storm|url=https://gamepress.gg/lostword/spell-card/wind-sign-miwa-plate-storm|access-date=2024-03-25|website=Touhou LostWord Wiki - GamePress|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
== See Also ==
